30	export class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
31	  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'
32	
33	  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
34	    return Promise.resolve({
35	      defaultOutput: getOutputPath(config),
36	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
37	      version: clientVersion,
38	      requiresEngines: [],

40	    })
41	  }

44	    const { config } = options.generator
45	    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
46	    const tsconfig = getTsconfig(outputDir)?.config
47	
48	    const target = config.runtime !== undefined ? parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown(config.runtime) : 'nodejs'
49	
50	    const generatedFileExtension =
51	      config.generatedFileExtension !== undefined ? parseGeneratedFileExtension(config.generatedFileExtension) : 'ts'
52	
53	    const importFileExtension =
54	      config.importFileExtension !== undefined
55	        ? parseImportFileExtension(config.importFileExtension)

32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',

36	/**

41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),

63	/**

65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )

85	  return options()
86	}

88	/**

90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */

93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`

98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`

106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`

134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(

141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),

143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {

152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)

204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path

209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)

212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled

232	}

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a thorough report:

## Comprehensive Prisma Generator Patterns Report

Based on my exploration of the Prisma monorepo, here's a detailed breakdown of the generator patterns:

---

### 1. **Core Type Definitions**

#### `GeneratorManifest` and `GeneratorOptions`
Located in `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`:

**GeneratorManifest** provides metadata about the generator:
```typescript
type GeneratorManifest = {
  prettyName?: string
  defaultOutput?: string
  denylists?: {
    models?: string[]
    fields?: string[]
  }
  requiresGenerators?: string[]
  requiresEngines?: EngineType[]
  version?: string
  requiresEngineVersion?: string
}
```

**GeneratorOptions** is passed during generation (from `@prisma/generator/src/configuration.ts`):
```typescript
type GeneratorOptions = {
  generator: GeneratorConfig
  otherGenerators: GeneratorConfig[]
  schemaPath: string
  dmmf: DMMF.Document
  datasources: DataSource[]
  datamodel: string
  version: string // version hash
  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
  noHints?: boolean
  allowNoModels?: boolean
  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
}
```

**GeneratorConfig** contains:
```typescript
interface GeneratorConfig {
  name: string
  output: EnvValue | null
  isCustomOutput?: boolean
  provider: EnvValue
  config: { [key: string]: string | string[] | undefined } // custom config
  binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
  previewFeatures: string[]
  sourceFilePath: string
}
```

---

### 2. **Generator Interface Pattern**

From `/packages/generator/src/generator.ts`:

```typescript
type Generator = {
  readonly name: string
  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}
```

Both `PrismaClientJsGenerator` and `PrismaClientTsGenerator` implement this interface.

---

### 3. **The `generatorHandler` Function**

Located in `/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`:

This is the core IPC handler that listens for JSON-RPC messages on stdin:

```typescript
export interface Handler {
  onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>
  onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest | Promise<GeneratorManifest>
}

export function generatorHandler(handler: Handler): void {
  // Listens on stdin for JSON-RPC 2.0 requests
  // Responds on stderr with JSON-RPC 2.0 responses
  // Supports two methods: 'generate' and 'getManifest'
}
```

**How it works:**
- Reads JSON-RPC 2.0 requests from stdin (one per line)
- Dispatches to handler methods (`onGenerate`, `onManifest`)
- Sends responses as JSON-RPC 2.0 on stderr
- Error handling includes code `-32000` with stack traces

---

### 4. **DMMF (Data Model Meta Format) Structure**

From `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`:

```typescript
type Document = {
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}

type Datamodel = {
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}

type Model = {
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}

type Field = {
  kind: 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  type: string
  nativeType?: [string, string[]]
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationName?: string
}
```

The Schema section contains GraphQL-like type definitions for queries, mutations, and inputs.

---

### 5. **Package.json Structure**

Both generator packages follow similar patterns:

**client-generator-js** (`/packages/client-generator-js/package.json`):
```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/client-generator-js",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": { "types": "./dist/index.d.ts", "default": "./dist/index.js" },
      "import": { "types": "./dist/index.d.mts", "default": "./dist/index.mjs" }
    }
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/client-common": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/internals": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/param-graph-builder": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/ts-builders": "workspace:*"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build"
  }
}
```

**Key patterns:**
- Dual format outputs (CJS + ESM via exports)
- Workspace dependencies only
- Build via `helpers/build.ts`
- No bin field (internal packages)

---

### 6. **Build Setup**

#### **helpers/compile/build.ts** (`/helpers/compile/build.ts`):
- Uses esbuild for bundling
- Supports both CJS and ESM outputs
- Watches for changes in dev mode (DEV=true)
- Emits TypeScript declarations via tscPlugin
- Loads tsconfig.build.json by default
- Platform: Node.js, Target: ES2022

#### **helpers/compile/configs.ts**:
```typescript
type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
  name?: string
  emitTypes?: boolean
  emitMetafile?: boolean
}

export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([
  { entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }
])

export const unbundledConfig = [
  { name: 'cjs', format: 'cjs', entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'], outdir: 'dist' },
  { name: 'esm', format: 'esm', entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'], outdir: 'dist' }
]
```

**Two build strategies:**
1. **adapterConfig** (generator-helper, client-generators): Single entry, bundled, CJS + ESM
2. **unbundledConfig** (generator package): Multiple entries, unbundled, each source file gets its own output

#### **tsconfig.build.json** pattern:
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "src"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

#### **Root tsconfig.build.regular.json**:
- Target: ES2022
- Module: commonjs
- Strict type checking enabled
- emitDeclarationOnly: true (types only, not used directly)
- Declaration maps enabled

---

### 7. **GeneratorProcess: Process-Based Execution**

From `/packages/generator-helper/src/GeneratorProcess.ts`:

Handles spawning external generator processes:

```typescript
export class GeneratorProcess {
  async init(): Promise<void> {}
  
  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest | null>
  generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
  
  stop(): void // Graceful shutdown with SIGTERM, then SIGKILL after 2s
}
```

**Process spawning:**
```typescript
if (isNode) {
  fork(pathOrCommand, [], {
    stdio: ['pipe', 'inherit', 'pipe', 'ipc'],
    execArgv: ['--max-old-space-size=8096']
  })
} else {
  spawn(pathOrCommand, { stdio: ['pipe', 'inherit', 'pipe'], shell: true })
}
```

**Communication:**
- Stdin: JSON-RPC 2.0 requests
- Stderr: JSON-RPC 2.0 responses (parsed line-by-line)
- Stdout: Inherited (generator output)
- Exit codes and signals handled explicitly

---

### 8. **Concrete Generator Implementations**

#### **PrismaClientJsGenerator** (`/packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts`):

```typescript
export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs // '@prisma/prisma-client-js'
  
  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    const defaultOutput = await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config)
    return {
      defaultOutput,
      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
      version: clientVersion,
      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion
    }
  }
  
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
    await generateClient({
      datamodel: options.datamodel,
      dmmf: options.dmmf,
      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
      datasources: options.datasources,
      outputDir,
      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
      typedSql: options.typedSql,
      // ... more options
    })
  }
}
```

#### **PrismaClientTsGenerator** (`/packages/client-generator-ts/src/generator.ts`):

Similar interface but:
- Name: `'prisma-client-ts'`
- Requires output path (no default)
- Parses tsconfig to infer module format
- Supports generatedFileExtension, importFileExtension, moduleFormat config
- Sets tsNoCheckPreamble: true

---

### 9. **Generator Registry Pattern**

From `/packages/client-generator-registry/src/registry.ts`:

```typescript
export class GeneratorRegistry {
  #generators = new Map<string, Generator>()
  
  add(generator: Generator) {
    this.#generators.set(generator.name, generator)
  }
  
  addAliased(name: string, generator: Generator) {
    this.#generators.set(name, generator)
  }
  
  toInternal(): IGeneratorRegistry {
    return Object.fromEntries(
      [...this.#generators.entries()].map(([name, generator]) => [
        name,
        { type: 'in-process', generator } // Marks as in-process vs external
      ])
    )
  }
}
```

**Registered generators** (`default.ts`):
```typescript
const defaultRegistry = new GeneratorRegistry()
defaultRegistry.add(new PrismaClientJsGenerator())
const tsGenerator = new PrismaClientTsGenerator()
defaultRegistry.add(tsGenerator)
defaultRegistry.addAliased('prisma-client', tsGenerator) // Alias for TS version
```

---

### 10. **JSON-RPC Communication Protocol**

From `/packages/generator-helper/src/json-rpc.ts`:

```typescript
type Request = {
  jsonrpc: '2.0'
  method: string
  params?: any
  id: number
}

type SuccessResponse = {
  jsonrpc: '2.0'
  result: any
  id: number
}

type ErrorResponse = {
  jsonrpc: '2.0'
  error: { code: number; message: string; data: any }
  id: number
}
```

**Methods supported:**
1. `generate` - calls `handler.onGenerate(params)`
2. `getManifest` - calls `handler.onManifest(params)` or returns null

---

### 11. **TypeScript Configuration Summary**

**Hierarchy:**
1. Root `tsconfig.json` - extends `tsconfig.utils.typecheck.json`
2. Root `tsconfig.build.regular.json` - compiler options
3. Package `tsconfig.build.json` - extends root + outDir/rootDir

**Key compiler options:**
- ES2022 target
- Strict mode
- Emit declarations only for build files
- Resolve extensions: `.ts`, `.js`, `.node`

---

### 12. **How DMMF is Accessed**

In generator options:
```typescript
options.dmmf: DMMF.Document // Full parsed schema
```

In `generateClient.ts`, it's converted:
```typescript
const dmmf = getPrismaClientDMMF(options.dmmf)
// Further transforms models, enums, relations for code generation
```

Generators access:
- `dmmf.datamodel.models` - models and types
- `dmmf.datamodel.enums` - enums
- `dmmf.schema` - GraphQL schema representation
- `dmmf.mappings` - model operation mappings

---

### 13. **Build and Distribution Flow**

1. **Source**: TypeScript in `packages/*/src/`
2. **Build**: `tsx helpers/build.ts` invokes esbuild
3. **Output**: 
   - CJS: `dist/index.js` + `dist/index.d.ts`
   - ESM: `dist/index.mjs` + `dist/index.d.mts`
4. **Package.json exports**: Dual-format with conditional exports
5. **Distribution**: Published to npm as workspace packages

---

### 14. **Key Dependencies Across Generator Packages**

- `@prisma/generator` - Core Generator interface
- `@prisma/dmmf` - Type definitions
- `@prisma/generator-helper` - JSON-RPC handler
- `@prisma/internals` - Internal utilities
- `@prisma/client-common` - Shared client logic
- `@prisma/ts-builders` - Code generation helpers
- `@prisma/param-graph-builder` - Query building utilities

---

### Summary Table

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Handler Pattern** | JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stderr |
| **Generator Interface** | `getManifest()` + `generate()` |
| **Build System** | esbuild with TypeScript + dual formats |
| **DMMF Access** | Via `options.dmmf: DMMF.Document` |
| **Process Execution** | fork (Node) or spawn (binaries) |
| **Configuration** | GeneratorConfig with output, provider, custom config |
| **Registry** | In-process vs external generator types |
| **Outputs** | CJS, ESM, TypeScript declarations |

This architecture enables both in-process generators (registered directly) and external generators (executed as separate processes) with a unified JSON-RPC interface.

#### **PrismaClientJsGenerator** (`/packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts`):

```typescript
export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs // '@prisma/prisma-client-js'
  
  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    const defaultOutput = await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config)
    return {
      defaultOutput,
      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
      version: clientVersion,
      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion
    }
  }
  
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
    await generateClient({
      datamodel: options.datamodel,
      dmmf: options.dmmf,
      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
      datasources: options.datasources,
      outputDir,
      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
      typedSql: options.typedSql,
      // ... more options
    })
  }
}
```

#### **PrismaClientTsGenerator** (`/packages/client-generator-ts/src/generator.ts`):

Similar interface but:
- Name: `'prisma-client-ts'`
- Requires output path (no default)
- Parses tsconfig to infer module format
- Supports generatedFileExtension, importFileExtension, moduleFormat config
- Sets tsNoCheckPreamble: true

---

### 9. **Generator Registry Pattern**

From `/packages/client-generator-registry/src/registry.ts`:

```typescript
export class GeneratorRegistry {
  #generators = new Map<string, Generator>()
  
  add(generator: Generator) {
    this.#generators.set(generator.name, generator)
  }
  
  addAliased(name: string, generator: Generator) {
    this.#generators.set(name, generator)
  }
  
  toInternal(): IGeneratorRegistry {
    return Object.fromEntries(
      [...this.#generators.entries()].map(([name, generator]) => [
        name,
        { type: 'in-process', generator } // Marks as in-process vs external
      ])
    )
  }
}
```

**Registered generators** (`default.ts`):
```typescript
const defaultRegistry = new GeneratorRegistry()
defaultRegistry.add(new PrismaClientJsGenerator())
const tsGenerator = new PrismaClientTsGenerator()
defaultRegistry.add(tsGenerator)
defaultRegistry.addAliased('prisma-client', tsGenerator) // Alias for TS version
```

---

### 10. **JSON-RPC Communication Protocol**

From `/packages/generator-helper/src/json-rpc.ts`:

```typescript
type Request = {
  jsonrpc: '2.0'
  method: string
  params?: any
  id: number
}

type SuccessResponse = {
  jsonrpc: '2.0'
  result: any
  id: number
}

type ErrorResponse = {
  jsonrpc: '2.0'
  error: { code: number; message: string; data: any }
  id: number
}
```

**Methods supported:**
1. `generate` - calls `handler.onGenerate(params)`
2. `getManifest` - calls `handler.onManifest(params)` or returns null

---

### 11. **TypeScript Configuration Summary**

**Hierarchy:**
1. Root `tsconfig.json` - extends `tsconfig.utils.typecheck.json`
2. Root `tsconfig.build.regular.json` - compiler options
3. Package `tsconfig.build.json` - extends root + outDir/rootDir

**Key compiler options:**
- ES2022 target
- Strict mode
- Emit declarations only for build files
- Resolve extensions: `.ts`, `.js`, `.node`

---

### 12. **How DMMF is Accessed**

In generator options:
```typescript
options.dmmf: DMMF.Document // Full parsed schema
```

In `generateClient.ts`, it's converted:
```typescript
const dmmf = getPrismaClientDMMF(options.dmmf)
// Further transforms models, enums, relations for code generation
```

Generators access:
- `dmmf.datamodel.models` - models and types
- `dmmf.datamodel.enums` - enums
- `dmmf.schema` - GraphQL schema representation
- `dmmf.mappings` - model operation mappings

---

### 13. **Build and Distribution Flow**

1. **Source**: TypeScript in `packages/*/src/`
2. **Build**: `tsx helpers/build.ts` invokes esbuild
3. **Output**: 
   - CJS: `dist/index.js` + `dist/index.d.ts`
   - ESM: `dist/index.mjs` + `dist/index.d.mts`
4. **Package.json exports**: Dual-format with conditional exports
5. **Distribution**: Published to npm as workspace packages

---

### 14. **Key Dependencies Across Generator Packages**

- `@prisma/generator` - Core Generator interface
- `@prisma/dmmf` - Type definitions
- `@prisma/generator-helper` - JSON-RPC handler
- `@prisma/internals` - Internal utilities
- `@prisma/client-common` - Shared client logic
- `@prisma/ts-builders` - Code generation helpers
- `@prisma/param-graph-builder` - Query building utilities

---

### Summary Table

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Handler Pattern** | JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stderr |
| **Generator Interface** | `getManifest()` + `generate()` |
| **Build System** | esbuild with TypeScript + dual formats |
| **DMMF Access** | Via `options.dmmf: DMMF.Document` |
| **Process Execution** | fork (Node) or spawn (binaries) |
| **Configuration** | GeneratorConfig with output, provider, custom config |
| **Registry** | In-process vs external generator types |
| **Outputs** | CJS, ESM, TypeScript declarations |

This architecture enables both in-process generators (registered directly) and external generators (executed as separate processes) with a unified JSON-RPC interface.

import { ReadonlyDeep } from './util'

export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}>

export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}>

export type OtherOperationMappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  read: string[]
  write: string[]
}>

export type DatamodelEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]
  dbName?: string | null
  documentation?: string
}>

export type SchemaEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: string[]
}>

export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
}>

export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}>

export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: string[]
}>
export type PrimaryKey = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string | null
  fields: string[]
}>
export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}>

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'
export type FieldLocation = 'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'

export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  /**
   * Describes the data type in the same the way it is defined in the Prisma schema:
   * BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
   */
  type: string
  /**
   * Native database type, if specified.
   * For example, `@db.VarChar(191)` is encoded as `['VarChar', ['191']]`,
   * `@db.Text` is encoded as `['Text', []]`.
   */
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string

import { ChokidarOptions, watch as createWatcher } from 'chokidar'
import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
import glob from 'globby'
import path from 'path'

import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
import { map } from '../blaze/map'
import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'

export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
  name?: string
  emitTypes?: boolean
  emitMetafile?: boolean
  outbase?: never // we don't support this
}

const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
  platform: 'node',
  target: 'ES2022',
  logLevel: 'error',
  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
  metafile: true,
} as const

/**
 * Apply defaults to the original build options
 * @param options the original build options
 */
const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
  format: 'cjs',
  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
  }),
  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
  ...options,
  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
  plugins: [
    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
    resolvePathsPlugin,
    fixImportsPlugin,
    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
    onErrorPlugin,
  ],
  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],

3	/** Maps Prisma scalar type names to their TypeScript equivalents. */
4	const SCALAR_TYPE_MAP: Readonly<Record<string, string>> = {
5	  String: 'string',
6	  Int: 'number',
7	  Float: 'number',
8	  Boolean: 'boolean',
9	  DateTime: 'Date',
10	  Json: 'unknown',
11	  Bytes: 'Uint8Array',

16	/** Maps a DMMF field to its TypeScript type representation. */
17	export function dmmfFieldToTsType(field: Field): string {
18	  let baseType: string
19	  switch (field.kind) {
20	    case 'scalar':
21	      baseType = SCALAR_TYPE_MAP[field.type] ?? 'unknown'
22	      break
23	    case 'enum':
24	    case 'object':
25	      baseType = field.type
26	      break
27	    default:
28	      baseType = 'unknown'
29	  }
30	  if (field.isList) {
31	    return `${baseType}[]`
32	  }
33	  if (!field.isRequired) {
34	    return `${baseType} | null`
35	  }
36	  return baseType
37	}
38	
39	/** Renders a Prisma enum definition as a TypeScript union type. */
40	export function renderEnum(enumDef: DatamodelEnum): string {
41	  const values = enumDef.values.map((v) => `'${v.name}'`).join(' | ')
42	  const doc = enumDef.documentation ? `/** ${enumDef.documentation} */\n` : ''
43	  return `${doc}export type ${enumDef.name} = ${values}`
44	}
45	
46	/** Renders a Prisma model definition as a TypeScript interface. */
47	export function renderModelInterface(model: Model): string {
48	  const doc = model.documentation ? `/** ${model.documentation} */\n` : ''
49	  const fields = model.fields
50	    .filter((f) => f.kind !== 'unsupported')
51	    .map((f) => {
52	      const fieldDoc = f.documentation ? `  /** ${f.documentation} */\n` : ''
53	      // Singular relation fields are optional since they may not be eagerly loaded
54	      const optional = f.kind === 'object' && !f.isList ? '?' : ''
55	      return `${fieldDoc}  ${f.name}${optional}: ${dmmfFieldToTsType(f)}`
56	    })
57	    .join('\n')
58	  return `${doc}export interface ${model.name} {\n${fields}\n}`
59	}
60	
61	/** Renders a filter input type for querying model records. */
62	export function renderWhereInput(model: Model): string {
63	  const fields = model.fields
64	    .filter((f) => f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum')
65	    .map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${dmmfFieldToTsType(f)}`)
66	    .join('\n')
67	  return `export interface ${model.name}WhereInput {\n${fields}\n}`
68	}
69	
70	/** Renders a unique-filter input type for addressing individual model records. */
71	export function renderWhereUniqueInput(model: Model): string {
72	  const uniqueFields = model.fields.filter(
73	    (f) => (f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum') && (f.isId || f.isUnique),
74	  )
75	  const fields = uniqueFields.map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${dmmfFieldToTsType(f)}`).join('\n')
76	  return `export interface ${model.name}WhereUniqueInput {\n${fields || '  [key: string]: unknown'}\n}`
77	}
78	
79	/** Renders an input type for creating a new model record. */
80	export function renderCreateInput(model: Model): string {
81	  const fields = model.fields
82	    .filter((f) => (f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum') && !f.isReadOnly)
83	    .map((f) => {
84	      // Auto-generated or default-valued fields are optional on create
85	      const optional = !f.isRequired || f.hasDefaultValue || f.isId ? '?' : ''
86	      return `  ${f.name}${optional}: ${dmmfFieldToTsType(f)}`
87	    })
88	    .join('\n')
89	  return `export interface ${model.name}CreateInput {\n${fields}\n}`
90	}
91	
92	/** Renders an input type for updating fields of an existing model record. */
93	export function renderUpdateInput(model: Model): string {

95	    .filter((f) => (f.kind === 'scalar' || f.kind === 'enum') && !f.isReadOnly && !f.isId)
96	    .map((f) => `  ${f.name}?: ${dmmfFieldToTsType(f)}`)
97	    .join('\n')
98	  return `export interface ${model.name}UpdateInput {\n${fields}\n}`
99	}
100	
101	/**
102	 * Converts a PascalCase model name to a kebab-case URL path with a plural suffix.
103	 *
104	 * Examples: `User` → `/users`, `BlogPost` → `/blog-posts`
105	 */
106	export function modelNameToPath(name: string): string {
107	  const kebab = name.split(/(?=[A-Z])/).filter(Boolean).join('-').toLowerCase()
108	  return `/${kebab}s`
109	}
110	
111	/** Converts a PascalCase model name to a camelCase property accessor name. */
112	export function modelNameToCamelCase(name: string): string {
113	  return name.charAt(0).toLowerCase() + name.slice(1)
114	}
115	
116	/** Renders the typed CRUD accessor object for one model as a `RestClient` class property. */
117	export function renderModelAccessor(model: Model): string {
118	  const propName = modelNameToCamelCase(model.name)
119	  const path = modelNameToPath(model.name)
120	  const idField = model.fields.find((f) => f.isId)
121	  // Fall back to first value of the where object for models without a single @id
122	  const idExpr = idField ? `where.${idField.name}` : 'Object.values(where)[0]'
123	  const n = model.name
124	
125	  return `  /** CRUD operations for the \`${n}\` model. */
126	  readonly ${propName} = {
127	    /** Returns a list of \`${n}\` records matching the given filter. */
128	    findMany: (params?: {
129	      where?: ${n}WhereInput
130	      skip?: number
131	      take?: number
132	      orderBy?: Partial<Record<keyof ${n}, 'asc' | 'desc'>>
133	    }): Promise<${n}[]> =>
134	      this.#request<${n}[]>('GET', '${path}', undefined, params as Record<string, unknown>),
135	
136	    /** Returns the \`${n}\` record matching the unique filter, or \`null\` if not found. */
137	    findUnique: (where: ${n}WhereUniqueInput): Promise<${n} | null> =>
138	      this.#request<${n} | null>('GET', \`${path}/\${${idExpr}}\`),
139	
140	    /** Creates and returns a new \`${n}\` record. */
141	    create: (data: ${n}CreateInput): Promise<${n}> =>
142	      this.#request<${n}>('POST', '${path}', data),
143	
144	    /** Updates and returns an existing \`${n}\` record. */
145	    update: (where: ${n}WhereUniqueInput, data: ${n}UpdateInput): Promise<${n}> =>
146	      this.#request<${n}>('PUT', \`${path}/\${${idExpr}}\`, data),
147	
148	    /** Deletes and returns the matching \`${n}\` record. */
149	    delete: (where: ${n}WhereUniqueInput): Promise<${n}> =>
150	      this.#request<${n}>('DELETE', \`${path}/\${${idExpr}}\`),
151	  }`
152	}

155	export function renderRestClientClass(models: readonly Model[]): string {

157	
158	  return `export class RestClient {
159	  readonly #baseUrl: string
160	  readonly #headers: Record<string, string>
161	
162	  constructor(options: { baseUrl: string; headers?: Record<string, string> }) {
163	    this.#baseUrl = options.baseUrl.replace(/\\/$/, '')
164	    this.#headers = options.headers ?? {}
165	  }
166	
167	  async #request<T>(
168	    method: string,
169	    path: string,
170	    body?: unknown,
171	    params?: Record<string, unknown>,
172	  ): Promise<T> {
173	    const url = new URL(\`\${this.#baseUrl}\${path}\`)
174	    if (params !== undefined) {
175	      for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(params)) {
176	        if (value !== undefined) {
177	          url.searchParams.set(key, JSON.stringify(value))
178	        }
179	      }
180	    }
181	    const response = await fetch(url.toString(), {
182	      method,
183	      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...this.#headers },
184	      body: body !== undefined ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,
185	    })
186	    if (!response.ok) {
187	      throw new Error(\`HTTP \${response.status}: \${response.statusText}\`)
188	    }
189	    return response.json() as Promise<T>

## `packages/generator-rest-client/` — New Package

### Files created

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `package.json` | `@prisma/generator-rest-client`, `bin: prisma-generator-rest-client`, workspace deps on `@prisma/dmmf`, `@prisma/generator`, `@prisma/generator-helper` |
| `tsconfig.json` | Extends `../../tsconfig.json`, includes all files (for IDE) |
| `tsconfig.build.json` | Extends `../../tsconfig.build.regular.json`, `outDir: dist` (for build) |
| `helpers/build.ts` | esbuild via `esmSplitCodeToCjs` plugin — same pattern as `generator-helper` |
| `src/index.ts` | Entry point; calls `generatorHandler` with `onManifest` + `onGenerate` |
| `src/generator.ts` | Resolves output path, creates the directory, writes `rest-client.ts` |
| `src/template.ts` | Pure string-template functions that render all TypeScript code |

### How it works

**`src/index.ts`** registers the generator with the Prisma JSON-RPC protocol via `generatorHandler`. `onManifest` declares the pretty name and a `defaultOutput` of `./generated/rest-client`.

**`src/generator.ts`** resolves the `output` path (supporting both literal values and `env("VAR")` references), creates the output directory with `mkdirSync`, then calls `generateContent` which sections the output file into enums → model interfaces → input types → the client class.

**`src/template.ts`** contains all pure rendering helpers:
- `dmmfFieldToTsType` — maps DMMF scalar/enum/object field kinds to TypeScript types (e.g. `DateTime → Date`, `BigInt → bigint`, relations → the model name, nullable fields → `Type | null`)
- `renderEnum` / `renderModelInterface` / `renderWhereInput` / `renderWhereUniqueInput` / `renderCreateInput` / `renderUpdateInput`
- `renderRestClientClass` — generates the full `RestClient` class with a private `#request<T>()` method (using `fetch` + `URL`) and one typed accessor property per model, each containing `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`

### Usage in a Prisma schema

```prisma
generator restClient {
  provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"
  output   = "./generated/rest-client"
}
```

After `prisma generate`, the output is a single `rest-client.ts` file that can be imported directly:

```typescript
import { RestClient } from './generated/rest-client/rest-client'

const client = new RestClient({ baseUrl: 'https://api.example.com' })
const users = await client.user.findMany({ where: { role: 'ADMIN' }, take: 10 })
const post  = await client.blogPost.create({ title: 'Hello', published: false })
```
